My work focuses on tightening language, strengthening brand voice, and translating complex ideas into messaging that feels clear, confident, and distinctive.

column16: Over the last 15 years, I’ve been translating strategy into story to help brands come to life and find their voice. I love that gooey liminal space where strategy and creative meet, and both in-house and in agency, my career has been built at the intersection of complexity and clarity. I started writing in financial services — American Express, Fidelity, Northwestern Mutual — learning to find the human story inside highly regulated, technical spaces. Compliance and legal love me. That discipline trained me to ask: what's the feeling underneath all this? It's a question I've carried into every engagement since, from early culture-shapers like Nike to biotech unicorns like Perfect Day, where I've helped translate genuinely difficult scientific concepts (hello, precision fermentation) into brand narratives that resonate. Whether I'm writing a creative territory, a brand manifesto, a script, or something else, narrative is ultimately about the connecting dots between ideas and between people.

column15: Brand Identity/Strategy, Content Strategy & Copy
column16:
Great brands need great copy. I write human-sounding copy that turns curious browsers into excited buyers.

It's easy to fill a page. But the voice behind a brand takes an intimate understanding of the interplay between brand, market, and the beliefs and identity of the people it's speaking to.

That's what separates great writing from mediocre writing. It's the difference between"this is for me" from something that gives you the ick. That subtle feeling when something's too polished and too obviously AI. 

I take the core DNA of a brand and imbue it with the richness of who they actually are. Contextualizing the brand as a persona and breathing life into it, so the right people feel understood in a way they couldn't quite put into words themselves.

That's the kind of writer I am, and that's what I'd bring to you. 

I've developed winning ad scripts & emails for brands. Please see here >>

column16:
My name is Daniel, but everybody calls me Don. People have been calling me that since I was a little boy. I’m a Design Director based in Buenos Aires, Argentina. I was born in Venezuela, but I’ve been living here for the past 12 years.

I graduated in advertising a long time ago, and I’ve been working in design and advertising for more than 20 years, first as a designer, then as a Head of Art, and eventually as a Design Director.

Throughout my career, I’ve had the chance to lead projects for global brands, developing digital campaigns, brand identities, and large-scale creative systems. Over the years I’ve basically worked on almost everything related to brands: ATL campaigns, BTL activations, digital marketing, social media, pitch decks, print, OOH, editorial design, brand guideline systems, loyalty programs, and branding projects from concept all the way to final execution.

My most recent role was Design Director at Superside, where I led the marketing creative design team. It was a very hands-on role because we were a relatively small team: three designers, one illustrator, and a couple of motion specialists. I collaborated closely with stakeholders across sales, growth, content, and product, while also mentoring designers and helping shape the creative vision for high-impact campaigns.

One area I’m particularly passionate about is integrating AI into the design process, not as a replacement for creativity, but as a creative partner. Over the past three years we’ve experimented with several tools. We started with Midjourney, then moved to Visual Electric, and currently we’re working a lot with Krea.

column16:
Once upon a time, we could reach potential customers by interrupting their programming with a brand's message. In an age of on-demand streaming and that "skip ads" button on Youtube, I suspect that the traditional 30-second ad spot is largely just annoying people. So, instead of interrupting the viewer's programming, I help brands tell the stories that become the programming. Now, ideally, those viewers will hit that "skip ads" button to get to what we've created. 

I spent 5 years co-creating and directing Nitto's 'On the Trail' off-road series. Instead of just talking about tire specs, we helped people overcome fear and build confidence to go off-road. That same approach works across automotive, maritime, and motorsports.

I live in the documentary-commercial space and aim to bring cinematic production quality to authentic human stories. My team is self-sufficient (capable of full-service production logistics), agency-fluent (MullenLowe, Cie Digital, D+G, Anima), and deeply experienced in this industry.

Clients include: Acura, Nitto, Road & Track, RM Sotheby's, Ferrari, Hagerty, Toyo, Porsche, Mercedes, and others.

VP Global Creative Director. The majority of my career has been in NYC—agency and brand side, with omni-channel expertise in global brands, advertising, design and 360º campaigns across Luxury, High Fashion, Beauty, Sport/Outdoors/Active Lifestyle, Spirits, Travel, Hospitality Home, D2C, Tech, Design Leadership and with culturally forward brands. Available and excited to work with new clients! Thanks for your consideration.

column15: Brand Identity/Strategy, Campaign/Advertising (Digital & Traditional), Content Strategy & Copy
column16:
I’m a writer, director, and artist driven simply by a nagging and strange sense of curiosity.
But at heart we could say that I am interested in people, interested in places, interested in culture - but that’s all to say that I am somehow inexplicably and magnetically drawn to the unfolding layers of experience.

Whether it’s conveying that curiosity and what I find in excavation through film, writing, painting, sculpting, therapeutic modalities, building, or care taking - it is all character based.
It is this beautiful, Russian doll kind of cataclysmic domino effect that, when you step back and look at the natural world as well as the man made one, you find a never ending well of amusement, of drama, of tragedy, of detail and nuance, of love, of surrealness that makes it all so god damn hilarious and attractive - and maybe sometimes a little beautifully sad as well.

I’ve brought that sensibility to working in advertising, film, television, and theater. A graduate of the prestigious LaGuardia High School for Music, Art, and the Performing Arts, an intern at the New Yorker, an actor in a traveling dinner theater troupe, a court appointed volunteer advocating for foster youth, an olive farmer in Spain, a documentarian following a Malian/Algerian Jazz band in the crosshairs of a geo-political struggle, an equine therapist, an assistant to a master ceramicist in the vast sprawl of Wonder Valley’s high desert, living on my motorcycle traveling the United States and Canada for several weeks at a time, a curator, an award-winning director - I find that limiting myself to any one moniker, any one title is not only a disservice to myself and what I’m capable of but also to what the world asks of me.

Outside of traditional production, I stay close to physical craft. In Wonder Valley, I work alongside potter Jonathan Cross, creating clay from raw earth, firing a wood kiln to 2500 degrees, and forming large clay blocks for sculpting and carving. It’s slow, elemental work — rooted in heat, patience, and intuition — and it continually informs how I think about making, imperfection, and material truth.

I have taken on facilitating equine therapy based experiences, with no agenda except to simply share the rich experience and lessons I have learned by blending my mindfulness practice with my horsemanship practice. My 6 year old thoroughbred mare, Willow, helps me out quite a bit here.

But let’s talk work experience for a moment. For over a  decade I have helped some of the biggest directors in the industry win large scale campaigns for global brands. Those directors include people like Darren Aronofsky, Alma Har’el, Todd Field, The Daniels, Lisa Cholodenko, Anthony Mandler, and a nearly never-ending list of names that might only be impressive to advertising nerds.

With Anthony I served as a creative writer and consultant on developing a tequila and whiskey brand. This project took us on week long trips across Mexico, Ireland, and England to embed in and soak up the culture, the food, the art, the people, and of course the origins of the whiskey and tequilas. It was not just a research trip but a cultural plunge into how a people, a country, an ethnicity express themselves through agriculture and, by proxy, the food and drink that lines its tables.

On the film and TV front I contributed creatively to the first two seasons of FX's award winning series, Justified. Asher, my first feature as writer, was produced by XYZ Films and stars Ron Perlman, Famke Jansen, Richard Dreyfus, Jaqueline Bisset, and Peter Facinelli. I followed that up with selling an original screenplay called Marauder to XYZ Films. Oh and I wrote a short film produced by Darren Aronofsky and directed by the great cinematographer, Matthew Libatique.

Recently I was awarded Best Director for my short film, Piano Man, on the festival circuit and premiered my art film, A Force Moves Against You, on Nowness.
In addition to that work I have developed TV shows and films across the board with actors, producers, and directors over the last 15 years.

Beyond all of that, I have, without any deliberate agenda, made extensive travel an important part of my personal syllabus. It’s simply been fueled by a craving to find a story in the most unlikely places, my interest in character and the roots of it, expressions through food, culture, color, texture, my grasp both of narrative, a keen photographic eye, and the beautiful strangeness of real human stories. The red sand dunes of Tadrart in the Sahara, a small jazz bar with great polenta fries in Bushwick, or the tragically beautiful soul of the Lakota Reservation in Pine Ridge, SD. In the retelling or capturing of those experiences I always look for the humor, something often forgotten but so critical, even in drama.

-J

I know how healthcare customers think, what they fear, and what moves them to act.

I’m Andrea, an award-winning Creative Director who turns brands into something you don’t automatically skip, mute, or roll your eyes at. I’ve worked with names you already know—Coca-Cola, Microsoft, American Express, Mercedes-Benz, L’Oréal and Sanofi—across agencies in the US and Spain, without losing my curiosity or my tolerance along the way.
I don’t think in formats. I think in how fast people leave. A billboard has three seconds to behave. Social has one. Creator content is basically speed dating.
Screens, streets, feeds or billboards—it’s all the same room, just different lighting. Big ideas, small details: if it needs explaining, it’s not ready yet. Attention is hard to get and easy to lose.
Advertising should make people stop.
And, if it doesn’t, it’s just expensive noise.

I'm a Creative Director and Copywriter with 13+ years on global brands—Zendesk, MERIT, TikTok, HP, Nike, TRESemmé, GORE-TEX, On Running. I've always believed you have to go places to get places, which is why I'm writing this from a medieval city in Sardinia.

I lead global ad campaigns from concept to copy: brand platforms, tone of voice, campaign strategy, digital-first storytelling—working independently or teamed with my AD partner of 10 years, André Blanco. A strategist as much as I am a storyteller, I care as much about why something's said as how it sounds.

As a brown female creative in an industry that's still catching up, I think we owe people better than content for content’s sake. The stories we tell shape what—and who—the world thinks of as normal. I'm drawn to work that sparks conversation, challenges norms, or introduces something genuinely new. 

I’m based in Cagliari and open to the right projects and meeting interesting new folk. 🌝

I’m a Creative Director, Art who knows how to make healthcare feel better. Pharma advertising is a space where shiny happy patients are the norm. I strive everyday to break that cycle. Here’s how:

I lead teams with a clear point of view, strong taste, and high standards. I concept across channels, guide ideas from brief to launch, and stay hands-on to make sure the work is smart, compliant, and emotionally relevant. I’m in my element when presenting to clients, collaborating with strategy and data partners, and keeping momentum and spirits up when timelines get tight.

I believe great healthcare work can be tow the line of regulation but still be ground breaking. I’m my toughest critic, and I know how to impart criticism constructively to inspire and not tear people down. I model my teams to care, challenge, and want to make a real impact.

If you’re looking for a hands-on CD who can lead, mentor, and elevate the work while keeping it grounded and effective, look no futher!

column15: Brand Identity/Strategy
column16:
From Chicago, now in Girona, Spain—I operate between North America and the EU. Both rad. Both different. I love Spain, and the Girona Gravel Girls community is amazing. 

I love to create. Plain and simple. I was a theater and film major, and I have not stopped creating. Canvas, vector, posters, bike frames, campaigns, painting a wall mural, creating community, and creating identity are all part of the same language to me. 

Brand identity isn’t a niche to say. It’s the privilege of getting in there with a client—asking the right questions, listening, figuring out what actually matters, then building something in collaboration.

I have worked with big brands before. Love them. Lately, though, I've had a soft heart for the smaller boutique operations that could use a marketing team, but they don't have the big-brand budget. That's where I've been coming in. And it's been a cool privilege. Thanks for this! Jen
